what: Call Claude to perform a deep quality audit of the knowledge entry
why: Structural checks alone cannot evaluate semantic quality, tag relevance, description accuracy, or uniqueness — only an LLM can do this reliably

success_handling.criteria: LLM returns valid JSON matching KnowledgeLlmAuditResult schema

error_handling.action: Return unavailable result — all findings set to warn with 'LLM unavailable' message

error_handling.scenario: LLM API call fails or returns invalid JSON
error_handling.action: Return unavailable result with error message in summary field

decisions.rationale: If the LLM is unavailable or fails, the audit still returns structural findings. The UI shows 'LLM unavailable' for all 10 checks rather than crashing the flow.
Sonnet provides the quality needed for accurate semantic evaluation without the cost of Opus. Haiku would miss subtle issues.
